messages.content: When directing to checkout for example I have 10 days booked at 50 pound a day and it's showing on the checkout as 'Qty 10, £50.00 each' But I want to change this to something like '10 nights @ £50 a night'

To change the text at the checkout to display '10 nights @ £50 a night', you should edit the code where it currently shows 'Qty 10, £50.00 each'. In most cases, you'd need to find the appropriate HTML element or template where this information is being displayed and modify the code. It would look like this: ```html
{{quantity}} nights @ £{{price}} a night
``` This will replace the old text with the new format you want for the checkout page. Make sure to backup your current code before making any changes, and make sure to test the changes on a staging environment, if available, before deploying it to your live site.
I am using stripe.redirectToCheckout so the checkout page is not controlled by me
lineitems no longer have a description in the latest api
When using stripe.redirectToCheckout how can I make it so that the payment isn't actually taken once the details are put in and it's just an intent until confirmed by an admin?
Once a payment has been created but not yet captured, how can I then store that payment intent in my backend/database and confirm at a later date?
How can I do this in nestjs?
How can I do it with nestjs, graphql and prisma? I want to also create a booking and user in the system at the same time the payment intent is created
Show me again but I need to get the payment intent id from a webhook from the stripe checkout page
